findings.title: formatDuration duplicated in 3 other files
findings.description: The new shared formatDuration util co-exists with local copies in InsightsExecutions.tsx:13, SkillUsageDashboard.tsx:72, and CodeReviewQualityDashboard.tsx:67 (formatDurationMs). The doc comment acknowledges drift but says migration is tracked as 'a separate refactor' without linking an issue, violating the project rule that TODOs must have an issue reference.

findings.title: formatNumber lacks a unit test file
findings.description: formatDuration was added alongside a test file at ui/src/utils/__tests__/formatDuration.test.ts, but formatNumber has no corresponding test. Both are new shared utilities exported from ui/src/utils/ and should have consistent test coverage.

findings.title: Test file not updated for rebuilt PRHeader
findings.description: PRHeader was rebuilt with a 4-tile meta grid (Opened, First review latency, Review runs, Findings summary), a diff-stats row, and new action buttons. The co-located test file was not updated — the existing 'renders PR header' test (line 170) only checks title and author. None of the new derived stats, formatting calls, or conditional branches are exercised by tests.

findings.title: TODO comment references the PR itself, not an issue
findings.description: The JSX comment says 'wiring ... to a real endpoint is item 4 on the punch list in the PR description (#1788)'. Referencing the PR itself as the tracking mechanism is not a durable issue reference — once the PR merges, the TODO becomes untrackable. Per project conventions, TODOs should reference a GitHub issue number.

findings.title: formatDuration drops hour remainder for day durations
findings.description: When a duration exceeds 24 hours, the function only shows whole days without the hour remainder (e.g. 36 hours renders as '1d' instead of '1d 12h'). This is inconsistent with the hours+minutes branch (line 37) which does include the minute remainder. The companion test file also lacks coverage for day+hour combinations, so the truncation went undetected.

findings.title: Hardcoded min-width: 200px on title
findings.description: crpd-header-title uses min-width: 200px as a raw pixel value while all other spacing/sizing in the file uses design tokens (var(--space-*), var(--font-size-*)). This is the only hardcoded pixel value in the file.
